name: Muhammad al-Asghar
title: Loyal Brother
shortDescription: Son of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala alongside his brother, the Master of Martyrs (AS), and was martyred.
fullStory:
Muhammad al-Asghar ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib was a son of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) whose mother was an umm-walad (maidservant). Some consider him to be Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr who was raised by Imam Ali, but most historical sources consider him Imam Ali's biological son.

He accompanied his brother Imam Hussain (AS) from Medina to Mecca and Karbala. On Ashura, after the martyrdom of many companions and Bani Hashem, Muhammad al-Asghar bravely went to the battlefield.

He recited poetry and fought fiercely. After killing a group of Kufans, a man from the Bani Aban ibn Darim tribe attacked and martyred him.

Muhammad al-Asghar's name is mentioned in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, testifying to the greatness and sacrifice of this loyal brother in defending Wilayah.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَمِيرِ المُؤْمِنِينَ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Muhammad ibn Amir al-Mu'minin...
